#  Enable Retainage on a Purchase Order or Subcontract 
##  Background 
Depending upon the accounting method set on a purchase order or subcontract, you can enable one or both retainage settings on the contract. To learn about setting the accounting method, see [How do I set the accounting method for a contract or funding?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-i-set-the-accounting-method-for-a-contract-or-funding)  
| Completed Work Retainage  | An agreed-upon percentage amount on a contract is withheld from a subcontractor to ensure the work is satisfactorily complete on the project.  | ![Icon Permissions Access Check](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/35af6c3878b6d54f67790e6b6491ebf1/713f0933defa9db9317328e2578e5bde/icon-permissions-access-check.png?fm=webp&h=12)  | ![Icon Permissions Access Check](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/35af6c3878b6d54f67790e6b6491ebf1/713f0933defa9db9317328e2578e5bde/icon-permissions-access-check.png?fm=webp&h=12)  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Stored Material Retainage  | An agreed-upon percentage amount allocated on a contract for stored materials to ensure sufficient materials are available to complete work.  | ![Icon Permissions Access Check](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/35af6c3878b6d54f67790e6b6491ebf1/713f0933defa9db9317328e2578e5bde/icon-permissions-access-check.png?fm=webp&h=12)  | ![Icon Permissions No Access X](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/cdaab801833cf04cee09180aa0f2fef6/a31acd56ba44c1549cf2605afba18b8d/red-x.png?fm=webp&h=12)  |  
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To enable retainage on a purchase order or subcontract:_
      * 'Admin' level permissions on the project's Commitments tool.  
OR
      * 'Read Only' or 'Standard' level permissions on the project's Commitments tool with the ['Create Work Order Contract', 'Create Purchase Order Contract', 'Update Work Order Contract', and/or 'Update Purchase Order Contract'](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/permissions-company/tutorials/grant-granular-permissions-in-a-project-permissions-template) granular permissions enabled on your permission template.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * To enable these settings on all of a project's new commitments, see [Configure Settings: Commitments](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-commitments).
    * To apply [sliding scale retention](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) to the commitment's invoices, see [Enable Sliding Scale Retention Rules on a Commitment's Invoices](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/enable-sliding-scale-retention-rules-on-subcontractor-invoices).


##  Prerequisites 
  * Create a Commitment
  * Set the contract's accounting method. See [How do I set the accounting method for a contract?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-i-set-the-accounting-method-for-a-contract-or-funding)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Commitments** tool.
  2. Click the **Contracts** tab.
  3. Locate the commitment to work with.
  4. Click the **Number** link to open the commitment.   
![Commitments Link Number](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/0ddc539065fc17460c73d7957b0c511d/de43d17f9824a82569df9a999c25962b/commitments-link-number.png?fm=webp)
  5. Click the **Advanced Settings** tab.
  6. Click **Edit**.  
![Subcontract Advanced Settings Edit Button](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b76cdacb79a750c13571dfc2ba1a363a/f96527dc80a88f99633119e0b4b4288e/subcontract-advanced-settings-edit-button.png?fm=webp)
  7. Scroll to the **Invoice** section and choose the options that correspond to the contract's accounting method (see [How do I set the accounting method for a contract?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-i-set-the-accounting-method-for-a-contract-or-funding)):
    1. **For contract's using the Unit/Quantity Based accounting method:  
** Place a mark in the **Enable Completed Work Retainage** checkbox. Contracts using the Unit/Quantity Based accounting method can only be used to bill for completed work. This method cannot be used to bill for stored materials.  
![Unit Qty Work Retainage](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8d89ebcf51c1ec1c07e46aab22c09fe7/15b422ab964413196f91443cfca1e045/unit-qty-work-retainage.png?fm=webp)  
OR
    2. **For contract's using the Amount-Based accounting method** :  
Place a mark in one or both checkboxes:  
![Amount Based Invoice Retainage](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/98a0ff0c4dc64ea16882b0565a5ced53/313ead0b7d858a2a85708da1099766ca/amount-based-invoice-retainage.png?fm=webp)
       * **Enable Completed Work Retainage**. Mark this checkbox to enable retainage for completed work. To learn more, see [Set or Release Retainage on a Subcontractor Invoice](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/set-or-release-retainage-on-a-subcontractor-invoice).   
AND/OR
       * **Enable Stored Material Retainage**. Mark this checkbox to enable retainage when billing for stored materials. To learn more, see [About Stored Materials on Invoices.](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/project-level/invoicing/tutorials/about-stored-materials-on-invoices)
    3. Click **Save**.


## See Also
  * [Enable Sliding Scale Retention Rules on Subcontractor Invoices](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/enable-sliding-scale-retention-rules-on-subcontractor-invoices)
  * [Set or Release Retainage on a Subcontractor Invoice (Legacy)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/set-or-release-retainage-on-a-subcontractor-invoice-legacy)
  * [Commitments - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/permissions)
title: Enable Retainage on a Purchase Order or Subcontract
description: How to enable Procore's default retainage options under the invoice settings for a purchase order or subcontract in the projects Commitments tool.
